draft_text: Most people will recover from these symptoms , and their reactions will lessen over time.
rationale: Extracted from remote_post-traumatic-stress-disorder-ptsd_34bb78526eb1.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: While many people who live through a dangerous event experience some symptoms initially, most do not develop PTSD—multiple factors play a part.
rationale: Extracted from remote_post-traumatic-stress-disorder-ptsd_34bb78526eb1.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: NIMH is supporting research to understand the factors that influence a person’s mental health after a potentially traumatic event.
rationale: Extracted from remote_post-traumatic-stress-disorder-ptsd_34bb78526eb1.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: NIMH-supported research is also studying ways to identify which trauma survivors are likely to recover naturally and which survivors may benefit from treatment.
rationale: Extracted from remote_post-traumatic-stress-disorder-ptsd_34bb78526eb1.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: NIMH-supported research is also focused on developing and improving mental health treatments that can help trauma survivors.
rationale: Extracted from remote_post-traumatic-stress-disorder-ptsd_34bb78526eb1.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: We’re also supporting research on ways to deliver effective treatment across different settings and populations.
rationale: Extracted from remote_post-traumatic-stress-disorder-ptsd_34bb78526eb1.html and tagged for variants review.
